content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : File                                          Date : September 28, 1989


From : FAIR, POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       John McLean

Subject : Advice Letter No. I-89-548

       Spoke to Susie Swatt regarding this advice request.  Told her
we had answered this question in Advice Letter No. A-89-499
(Olson).  Also informed her that Advice Letter No. A-89-358
(Roberti) prohibited the establishment of a recall committee until
an officer had been served with the recall petition.

       I faxed her copies of those letters and she indicated that
the letters would satisfy their request.

JM/aa

SACRAMENTO OFFICE
STATE CAPITOL
P.O. BOX 942849
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814-0001
(916) 445-7418

Assembly
California Legislature

DISTRICT OFFICE
1501 NORTH HARBOR BOULEVARD
SUITE 201
FULLERTON, CA 92635
(714) 738-5853
(213) 697-0603

ROSS JOHNSON
ASSEMBLYMAN SIXTY-FOURTH DISTRICT

ASSEMBLY REPUBLICAN LEADER

September 21, 1989

Mr. John McLean
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear John:

I would appreciate confirmation on advice you have given to my
staff regarding recall campaigns. In verbal conversations, you
indicated that recall campaigns are not subject to the off-year
fundraising ban, which goes into effect on October 2, 1989.

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

ROSS JOHNSON
Assembly Republican Leader

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 27, 1989

Honorable Ross Johnson
Assemblymember, Sixty-Fourth District
Assembly Republican Leader
State Capitol
P.O. Box 942849
Sacramento, CA 94249-0001

Re: Letter No. 89-548

Dear Assemblymember Johnson:

We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice under the Political Reform Act on September 22, 1989. Your letter has been assigned to John McLean for response. If you have any questions, you may contact him directly at (916) 322-5901.

If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. A confirming response will be released after it has gone through our approval process. If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you shortly to advise you. In such cases, the normal analysis, review and approval process will be followed.

You should be aware that your letter and our response are public records which may be disclosed to any interested person upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

KED:plh:confadv1

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
